title: Operations — Audit Pipeline Broken (CloudTrail / CW Logs / S3 archive)
description: An engineer configures `AuditTrail` (M9) but the consumer-supplied SecureBucket archive bucket policy doesn't grant CloudTrail write access; or the CW Logs group's KMS key is rotated without granting CT IAM role kms:Encrypt; or an attacker calls StopLogging on the trail before exfiltrating. Threats focus on the integrity, availability, and tamper-resistance of the audit pipeline.
actors: External Attacker (post-compromise; objective: cover tracks via StopLogging)
Compromised privileged AWS principal with `cloudtrail:*` (could disable trail)

stride.name: Spoofed source on StopLogging
stride.description: An attacker with `cloudtrail:StopLogging` disables the trail before lateral movement. Mitigation: `IdentityAlarms` event 5 (`cloudtrail-tampered`) fires on `StopLogging` / `DeleteTrail` / `UpdateTrail`. Pair with SCP denying those actions to non-IaC principals.

stride.name: Tampered S3 archive (without log-file validation)
stride.description: An attacker with `s3:PutObject` on the archive bucket modifies historical log files. Mitigation: `enableLogFileValidation: true` (M9 invariant) makes any post-hoc modification cryptographically detectable via `aws cloudtrail validate-logs`. SecureBucket's BucketOwnerEnforced + TLS-only policy prevent the tamper at the bucket-policy level.

stride.name: Repudiation via missing CW Logs delivery
stride.description: If the CT-to-CWL IAM role policy lacks `logs:CreateLogStream` for the specific log group ARN, the trail emits to S3 but not to CW Logs — `IdentityAlarms` are silent. Mitigation: M9's `RolePolicy` is scoped to `${logGroup.arn}:*`; consumer can verify with `aws cloudtrail get-trail-status`.

stride.name: Information disclosure via unencrypted CW Logs group
stride.description: If a consumer points `IdentityAlarms.trailLogGroupName` at a log group that ISN'T `kmsKeyId`-set, CloudTrail events at rest in CW Logs are readable by any cross-account principal with `logs:GetLogEvents`. Mitigation: M9 sets `kmsKeyId` on the log group; M10's `O_AUDIT_2` enforces it at preview for every `/aws/cloudtrail/*` group.

stride.name: Denial via S3 archive bucket policy regression
stride.description: If the archive bucket policy is updated to deny CloudTrail (e.g. via a typo in a Pulumi update), the trail starts failing delivery. Mitigation: `aws cloudtrail get-trail-status` exposes `LatestDeliveryError`; pair with a CloudWatch alarm watching the trail's delivery error count.

stride.name: Elevation via cross-account access to the audit archive
stride.description: An attacker with cross-account read on the archive bucket can correlate IaC-author identity with workload behavior. Mitigation: SecureBucket's BucketOwnerEnforced + VPC endpoint policy + per-account KMS keys.

abuseCases.title: Archive bucket policy regression breaks delivery
abuseCases.description: Mitigation: M9 trail-status check + alarm on delivery errors.
